Lore Item
Slot: FEET
AC: 10
STA: +5
WT: 3.6 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Ivy Etched Boots
Lore Item
Slot: FEET
AC: 10
STA: +5
WT: 3.6 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):100, 175, 100
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:2 pp 7 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:120

Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:42:49
Page Updated:Thu May 4th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 575pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 30

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Ivy Etched Boots

Zone(s) Found In:

▲ Page top
Faction Information
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2002 at 12:20 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361983788
2 posts
Although I know there are better boots out there, I am doing this quest for the fun of it. However, I have discovered something in the process. You have to con amiably to Lord Searfire to get the cauldron. Indifferently won't work. So, I am killing Shadowed Men to raise Solusek Ro faction. Also, you need the same type of faction with the Priestess in the Kaladim Cleric's Guild. The only way I remember to raise faction with Kaladim Citizens is to free Dwarf slaves in Crushbone.
▲ Page top
RE: Faction Information
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2002 at 5:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758993467
4 posts
Bone Quest comes to mind, check Faction list on left side of page. Good Hunting

These are crap

79 posts
For the trouble you have to go through, I will stick with Dwarven Work Boots, I dont too much care about 1 less AC, I have a pile of AC jewelry anyway.

RE: These are crap
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2002 at 3:58 AM Rating: Decent
Kundow
79 posts
Yeah, I forgot about the STA-->HP thing at the time, oops! I was just thinking about the Ranger spell Invigor, which keeps your STA bar from dropping, oh well. /shrug
I got my DWB now, and will most likely stick with them, I like the DEX. Good stuff.
▲ Page top
RE: These are crap
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2002 at 4:26 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759473426
9 posts
These aren't crap really. STA is a needed stat, because sta = hp. HP > Ac. BTW, DWBs have two less ac, not one.

QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2002 at 1:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758106980
222 posts
LOL - found them on a merchant in Neriak for 36 plat (think someone was clearing out a character - found Ulthork hide arms and Woe on same merchant) - gotta descide now whether to sell them or hold onto them in case I want to do a ranger at some point.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 25 2001 at 6:07 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) These things suck...
Hope you got the Rabbit Hide Boots...
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 3:50 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The Rabbit Hide Boots (or Rabbit fur...not sure atm cause i onlt have one com and can't look at them while writing this)that dropped at the "GM" Events starting this past Halloween are Far better than these. If you're a ranger and had your character to at least level 10 when the events happened you can easily get these. My ranger is only level 12 and he managed to pick up the Collar, Cloak, Boots, Hat, and Earring. From what i hear the Belt, Veil and Arms were only dropping from the higher ones in WC, EK, and N Ro so i didn't get to get those...but i hope that all of you done the same as i did and picked up the Rabbit boots along with the other stuff, all you needed to be was a good navigator...everyone else done the work for ya ; )

May Tunare Lead You To Grobb...
▲ Page top
RE: Hope you got the Rabbit Hide Boots...
QuoteReply
# Feb 06 2002 at 10:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250101969
15 posts
Lucky, i only got the neck and earing
▲ Page top
RE: Hope you got the Rabbit Hide Boots...
QuoteReply
# Nov 07 2001 at 3:43 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The Rabbit Hide Boots aren't bad at all, except for one slight detail... their AC. 5, to be exact. YUCK. For that reason alone, there's just no way I could convince myself to wear those instead of my Ivy Boots. Rangers die enough as it is.

I'll take that 5-point stamina boost over the +4 to dexterity, wisdom, and agility any day. That's what Forest Loops are for. A lot of people still don't know that if your stamina is 100 or higher, you won't suffer stat loss when your yellow stamina bar is depleted, and you can run at full speed. The only thing you can't do is jump.
▲ Page top
RE: Hope you got the Rabbit Hide Boots...
QuoteReply
# May 01 2002 at 5:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251599071
62 posts
Man your nuts. 5 sta? Over dex/agi/wis? Maybe at lev 20 the ivy is better but not after that. Sta is way to easy to get of 100. Mine is 117 and I never tried to raise it. Sta is on so many items it will go up over 100 without trying. Dex and Wis on the other hand are always desired as a Ranger.
▲ Page top
RE: Hope you got the Rabbit Hide Boots...
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 3:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762323414
109 posts
Oh ya, forgot to mention, I DIDN'T DIE while getting all the items =D All i did was keep the BR on track at all times and be where he wasn't.
▲ Page top
Sufficient lvl
QuoteReply
# Oct 29 2001 at 1:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758373017
44 posts
Im now lvl 46 and probaly ivy quests are 40+, not 30 as its mentioned here. Or u need really good group at 30, now i did easily tunic, bracer and sleeves with my 50 druid friend
▲ Page top
RE: Sufficient lvl
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2002 at 7:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760155719
Scholar
16 posts
I'm a level 22 ranger and I have done all but the legging and glove quests. They aren't terribly hard so at level 46 you should be able to breeze right through them! They just require time, luck, and quite a bit of platinum (seriously why do you need to buy gems for a quested item?). Of course at lower levels you need much more luck! Most of the items used in these quests (most quests really) are no drop and worthless to those not doing the quests. The Rune of Ivy is often left to rot when people hunt Thistle Underbrush for his forest loop. Just ask nicely and most people will be more than happy to let you loot quest items if they don't need them. Be safe and may Tunare guide your on you journeys.

Sollaiel Sunbringer
Ranger of the 22nd hunt
Vazaelle
▲ Page top
RE: Sufficient lvl
QuoteReply

My characters name is Huch i need these boots so please send me a tell if you got them. am on all the time, nights and weekends

very nice
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 12:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759600094
19 posts
I think this is the only piece of ivy etched I like. I usually wear ry'gor stuff..why? because the stats are better and it looks very nice. Oh and there aren't no ry'gor feet piece either =P. The 5sta is great because my woodelf starting sta was low..75 to be exact. Newaz every melee stat is important for a ranger (str, sta, dex, and agil) Oh and the 10ac is hard to find in a pair of boots.
▲ Page top
RE: very nice
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 12:12 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
These are definitely good boots for stamina-starved rangers such as my alt. But if you want a pair of boots with the Ry'Gorr texture, consider Sentry Boots. Stats are 13ac, 2agi and 3sta (maybe 3agi and 2sta, /shrug) and a couple of minor saves. Any class that can wear banded can wear Sentry. And they're not THAT expensive; I've bought them for as low as 250, but even 500 would be a good value.
